metadata.title: Use Azure Resource Manager templates in Azure Stack | Microsoft Docs
metadata.description: Learn how to use Azure Resource Manager templates in Azure Stack to provision resources.
metadata.author: sethmanheim
metadata.ms.author: sethm

content.markdown: *Applies to: Azure Stack integrated systems and Azure Stack Development Kit*
content.text: *Applies to: Azure Stack integrated systems and Azure Stack Development Kit*

content.markdown: *Applies to: Azure Stack integrated systems and Azure Stack Development Kit*
content.text: Applies to: Azure Stack integrated systems and Azure Stack Development Kit

content.markdown: Applies to: Azure Stack integrated systems and Azure Stack Development Kit
content.text: Applies to: Azure Stack integrated systems and Azure Stack Development Kit

content.markdown: You can use Azure Resource Manager templates to deploy and provision all the resources for your application in a single, coordinated operation. You can also redeploy templates to make changes to the resources in a resource group.
content.text: You can use Azure Resource Manager templates to deploy and provision all the resources for your application in a single, coordinated operation. You can also redeploy templates to make changes to the resources in a resource group.

content.markdown: You can use Azure Resource Manager templates to deploy and provision all the resources for your application in a single, coordinated operation. You can also redeploy templates to make changes to the resources in a resource group.
content.text: You can use Azure Resource Manager templates to deploy and provision all the resources for your application in a single, coordinated operation. You can also redeploy templates to make changes to the resources in a resource group.

content.markdown: These templates can be deployed with the Microsoft Azure Stack portal, PowerShell, the command line, and Visual Studio.
content.text: These templates can be deployed with the Microsoft Azure Stack portal, PowerShell, the command line, and Visual Studio.

content.markdown: These templates can be deployed with the Microsoft Azure Stack portal, PowerShell, the command line, and Visual Studio.
content.text: These templates can be deployed with the Microsoft Azure Stack portal, PowerShell, the command line, and Visual Studio.

content.markdown: The following quickstart templates are [available on GitHub](https://aka.ms/azurestackgithub):
content.text: The following quickstart templates are [available on GitHub](https://aka.ms/azurestackgithub):

content.markdown: The following quickstart templates are
[available on GitHub](https://aka.ms/azurestackgithub)

Use the PowerShell [Desired State Configuration](/powershell/dsc/overview/overview) (DSC) extension to [create a SharePoint Server 2013 farm](https://github.com/Azure/AzureStack-QuickStart-Templates/tree/master/sharepoint-2013-non-ha) that includes the following resources:
content.text: Use the PowerShell [Desired State Configuration](/powershell/dsc/overview/overview) (DSC) extension to [create a SharePoint Server 2013 farm](https://github.com/Azure/AzureStack-QuickStart-Templates/tree/master/sharepoint-2013-non-ha) that includes the following resources:

content.markdown: Use the PowerShell
[Desired State Configuration](/powershell/dsc/overview/overview)
(DSC) extension to
[create a SharePoint Server 2013 farm](https://github.com/Azure/AzureStack-QuickStart-Templates/tree/master/sharepoint-2013-non-ha)
that includes the following resources:
A virtual network
Three storage accounts
Two external load balancers

content.text: One virtual machine (VM) configured as a domain controller for a new forest with a single domain

content.text: One virtual machine (VM) configured as a domain controller for a new forest with a single domain
One VM configured as a SQL Server 2014 stand-alone server

content.text: One VM configured as a SQL Server 2014 stand-alone server

content.markdown: One VM configured as a one-machine SharePoint Server 2013 farm
content.text: One VM configured as a one-machine SharePoint Server 2013 farm

content.markdown: Use the PowerShell DSC extension to configure an existing virtual machine Local Configuration Manager (LCM) and register it to an Azure Automation Account DSC Pull Server.
content.text: Use the PowerShell DSC extension to configure an existing virtual machine Local Configuration Manager (LCM) and register it to an Azure Automation Account DSC Pull Server.

content.title: Create a virtual machine from a user image

content.markdown: [Create a virtual machine from a custom user image](https://github.com/Azure/AzureStack-QuickStart-Templates/tree/master/101-vm-create-from-customimage). This template also deploys a virtual network (with DNS), public IP address, and a network interface.
content.text: [Create a virtual machine from a custom user image](https://github.com/Azure/AzureStack-QuickStart-Templates/tree/master/101-vm-create-from-customimage). This template also deploys a virtual network (with DNS), public IP address, and a network interface.

content.markdown: [Create a virtual machine from a custom user image](https://github.com/Azure/AzureStack-QuickStart-Templates/tree/master/101-vm-create-from-customimage)
content.text: Create a virtual machine from a custom user image

content.markdown: . This template also deploys a virtual network (with DNS), public IP address, and a network interface.
content.text: . This template also deploys a virtual network (with DNS), public IP address, and a network interface.

content.markdown: [Deploy a Windows VM](https://github.com/Azure/AzureStack-QuickStart-Templates/tree/master/101-simple-windows-vm) that includes a virtual network (with DNS), public IP address, and a network interface.
content.text: [Deploy a Windows VM](https://github.com/Azure/AzureStack-QuickStart-Templates/tree/master/101-simple-windows-vm) that includes a virtual network (with DNS), public IP address, and a network interface.

content.markdown: [Deploy a Windows VM](https://github.com/Azure/AzureStack-QuickStart-Templates/tree/master/101-simple-windows-vm)
that includes a virtual network (with DNS), public IP address, and a network interface.
content.text: that includes a virtual network (with DNS), public IP address, and a network interface.

content.markdown: To cancel a running template deployment, use the [Stop-AzureRmResourceGroupDeployment](/powershell/module/azurerm.resources/stop-azurermresourcegroupdeployment) PowerShell [cmdlet](/powershell/developer/cmdlet/cmdlet-overview).
content.text: To cancel a running template deployment, use the [Stop-AzureRmResourceGroupDeployment](/powershell/module/azurerm.resources/stop-azurermresourcegroupdeployment) PowerShell [cmdlet](/powershell/developer/cmdlet/cmdlet-overview).

content.markdown: To cancel a running template deployment, use the
content.text: To cancel a running template deployment, use the

content.markdown: [Stop-AzureRmResourceGroupDeployment](/powershell/module/azurerm.resources/stop-azurermresourcegroupdeployment)
PowerShell
[cmdlet](/powershell/developer/cmdlet/cmdlet-overview)
[Deploy templates with the portal](azure-stack-deploy-template-portal.md)
[Deploy templates with PowerShell](azure-stack-deploy-template-powershell.md)
[Deploy templates with Visual Studio](azure-stack-deploy-template-visual-studio.md)
